Reason: The paper (Small, 2024) provides a complete, extractable preparation workflow for PRP-loaded GelMA hydrogel microspheres. Sections 2.2.1 and 2.2.2 describe all essential steps: PRP isolation from human blood, GelMA synthesis from type A gelatin, GelMA solution preparation, microfluidic microsphere fabrication, UV crosslinking, washing, and PRP loading. Key parameters (masses, volumes, temperatures, flow rates, centrifugation conditions) are explicitly reported.

Source.pdf_file: paper/GELMA/Small - 2024 - Yuan - Injectable GelMA Hydrogel Microspheres with Sustained Release of Platelet‐Rich Plasma for the.pdf
Source.markdown_file: markdown/Small - 2024 - Yuan - Injectable GelMA Hydrogel Microspheres with Sustained Release of Platelet‐Rich Plasma for the.md

Materials: Human venous blood: 9 mL per preparation
Sodium citrate anticoagulant: 1 mL per 9 mL blood
Thrombin (Sigma Aldrich Cat: T6884): for PRP activation
Instruments: Centrifuge (for 15 mL tubes, up to 2000 × g)
Microfluidic device (capillary glass, outlet Ø 0.1 mm, channel Ø 0.5 mm)
Containers: Cryogenic storage vial (for PRP and final composite)

Operations.Description: Combine 1 mL sodium citrate anticoagulant with 9 mL venous blood. Centrifuge at 300 × g for 20 min at room temperature.

Object: stratified blood upper and junction layers
Operations.Description: Carefully remove the yellowish upper layer and the junction layer between upper and lower fractions.
Re-centrifuge collected fraction at 300 × g for 20 min at room temperature. Recover bottom liquid as PRP.
Adjust platelet concentration to 1×10^9 mL−1. Mix PRP with 10% CaCl2 and thrombin in 9:1 volume ratio.
Incubate at 37 °C for 4 h.
Centrifuge at 2000 × g for 10 min at room temperature.
Filter through 0.22 μm filter.
Freeze at −80 °C for subsequent experiments.
Dissolve 2 g type A gelatin in 20 mL PBS at 60 °C with stirring.
Add 1.6 mL MA dropwise at 1.0 mL h−1 for 3 h under vigorous stirring at 60 °C.
Add 80 mL prewarmed PBS to dilute the reaction mixture.
Dialyze against water for one week to remove unreacted components.
Filter through 0.22 μm filter.
Freeze at −80 °C overnight.
Lyophilize to obtain purified GelMA solid.
Dissolve 0.7 g lyophilized GelMA in 10 mL PBS containing 0.5% w/v photoinitiator at 60 °C.
Filter through 0.22 μm filter for sterilization.
Mix GelMA precursor solution with PRP to form the dispersed phase for microfluidics.
Prepare continuous phase: paraffin oil with 5% Span 80 surfactant.
Inject dispersed phase at 1.2 mL h−1 and continuous phase at 12 mL h−1 into microfluidic device at 20 °C. Form microspheres by shear force.
Cure collected microdroplets by UV irradiation to crosslink GelMA.

UV_device: not specified: wavelength and duration not reported in source

Operations.Description: Wash microspheres with double-distilled water and centrifuge. Repeat several times.
Add washed microspheres to hydrogels mixed with desired concentration of PRP to obtain PRP@GelMA-M composite.

Missing information: UV irradiation wavelength and intensity not specified
Dialysis medium and exchange frequency not detailed
Photoinitiator exact chemical identity not explicitly named
Microsphere washing centrifugation speed and time not specified
PRP-to-GelMA volumetric ratio in dispersed phase not explicitly given
